assertions.evidence: @TableTest(""" appears at the start of the method

assertions.text: Table has at least one column name ending with '?' (e.g. 'Discount?' or 'Expected?')
assertions.evidence: Column header 'Discount?' is present in the table

assertions.text: Table has exactly 3 data rows (GOLD, SILVER, BRONZE)
assertions.evidence: Three data rows present: 'Gold customer | GOLD | 20', 'Silver customer | SILVER | 10', 'Bronze customer | BRONZE | 0'

assertions.text: Test method body contains only one assertEquals (or equivalent assertion) — not three
assertions.evidence: Method body contains only: assertEquals(discount, discountService.calculate(customerTier, 100));

assertions.text: Test method body contains no if or switch statements
assertions.evidence: Method body is a single assertEquals statement with no conditional logic

assertions.text: Table has a scenario/description column as the leftmost column
assertions.evidence: Leftmost column header is 'Scenario' with values 'Gold customer', 'Silver customer', 'Bronze customer'

assertions.text: Test method has either a @DisplayName annotation or a method name that reads as a clear, descriptive title when converted from camelCase/snake_case
assertions.evidence: Method name 'calculatesDiscountByTier' converts to 'Calculates Discount By Tier', a clear descriptive title

assertions.text: @Description is present and provides context beyond what the table rows already express
assertions.evidence: No @Description annotation is present in the output

assertions.text: Annotations appear in order: @DisplayName (if present), @Description (if present), @TableTest — not any other order
assertions.evidence: Only @TableTest is present; no @DisplayName or @Description, so the ordering rule is satisfied

assertions.text: @Description text uses a text block (triple-quoted string """) when the text is long, not string concatenation with +
assertions.evidence: No @Description annotation is present in the output
